posts.cooked:
Got my Z-list shipment last week. Noticed two small changes:

  1. Capsule: The short/silvery capsules on the Ridge wines are instantly recognizable. The ATP wines always have “ATP”
    embossed on the top of the capsule. In this shipment, I noticed the Geyserville (but not the others) had “Ridge Vineyards”
    embossed on the capsule. Don’t know if all the rest in the future will have this or not.
  2. Labels: I also noticed that the type font appeared to be in a slightly bolder (but not bold face)/darker text.

I am, of course, going to complain about these changes to MarkVernon & PaulDraper. They should know by now that
old folks can’t deal w/ changes very readily.

One thing that’s always perplexed my about the Ridge labels is where they put the bottling date. The format varies all over the map,
even amongst the same winemaker. Sometimes it’s “October 2014”, sometimes “Oct 2014”, sometimes “Oct '14”,
sometimes “8/2014”, sometimes “8/'14”. I’ve commented on this to MarkVernon several times and he just grins, nods his head “yes”,
and gives me this $hit-eating grin. I’m beginning to think that maybe this is intentional, since Ridge is so methodical/precise in their
labeling practices.

Anyway…not a biggie in the whole scheme of things. Only important to folks who have followed Ridge from the very start.

Tom

posts.name: Josh Grossman
posts.cooked: I would imagine this makes Monte Bello (and others) mighty hard to counterfeit.

posts.name: M. Dildine
posts.cooked:
The “very start”? I‘m close Tom, and I noticed too.

What’s with this radical change?

posts.name: Markus S
posts.cooked: Aren’t changes a part of life?

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked: You had me worried. As long as they only change what is on the bottle and not what is in the bottle, I am ok.

M. Dildine:

The “very start”? I‘m close Tom, and I noticed too.
What’s with this radical change?

Sent my comments to Eric:

Eric:

We have made a couple enhancements to the label and capsule. Going forward all the wines will have either ATP or RIDGE VINEYARDS embossed capsules. This Spring release shipment had a mix of both due to timing of bottling and having the new capsules produced in Spain and delivered. They are here now and in widespread use for bottling. The other change, you won’t know, is that John and I are now using only TCA-free GC/MS tested corks. There was a breakthrough of technology with Amorim Cork group to test every single cork using automated equipment. The process is called “Icon” and it adds some expense to the already expensive cork, but tests and confirms absence of TCA cork taint.
Regarding the labels, we have started to use a different paper that has a water barrier layer integrated. That helps give the label greater paper strength and hold up better to condensation. It prints differently than the former paper and requires going slightly heavier on the print. As we moved to higher font size for the back labels, it became even more necessary to make this change so that the text printed with better resolution. In my opinion, with my now aging eyes, made labels that are so much easier to read.
I know change can be a concern, but these are minor adjustments to the bottle aesthetics and the cork closure quality. I can guarantee what has not changed is what’s inside the bottle.

To tell the truth, I very/very rarely encounter a corked/TCA-infected btl from Ridge. Their cork quality control is one of
the best in the business.
We tasted the new Ridges last night. The Geyserville '16 is one of the best. Very perfumed & exotic, not the usual Geyserville.
The PasoRobles/BeniDusi is one of the best Dusi’s in a long time.
Tom

posts.cooked:

Tom
